task_completion.description: Subsurface application is open and dive data is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface main window is visible with a populated list of dives.

task_completion.description: The agent accesses the unit settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Preferences/Settings window is open to the Units tab in Subsurface, OR the Subsurface configuration file is open in a text editor showing the [Units] section.

task_completion.description: Depth/Length is set to feet and Pressure to psi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GUI, 'feet' and 'psi' are selected. If editing the config file, 'length=1' and 'pressure=1' are visible.

task_completion.description: Temperature is set to Fahrenheit and Weight to lbs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GUI, 'Fahrenheit' and 'lbs' are selected. If editing the config file, 'temperature=1' and 'weight=1' are visible.

task_completion.description: Volume is set to cu ft.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GUI, 'cu ft' is selected. If editing the config file, 'volume=1' is visible.

task_completion.description: The main Subsurface window displays the dive data using imperial units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive list or dive details pane in Subsurface shows measurements in imperial units (e.g., 'ft' for depth, 'psi' for pressure, '°F' for temperature, 'lbs' for weight, 'cuft' for volume).

integrity.description: The agent verified the changes in the actual Subsurface application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshots show the Subsurface GUI reflecting the updated units, rather than just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual application settings rather than tampering with the dive data file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with the Preferences menu or the Subsurface configuration file, and did not manually edit the numerical values or unit strings inside the dives.ssrf file to fake the conversion.

privileged_info_for_vlm: In the Subsurface configuration file (.conf), the imperial units map to the integer value '1'. Specifically: length=1 (Feet), pressure=1 (PSI), temperature=1 (Fahrenheit), weight=1 (Lbs), and volume=1 (CuFt).
